Empires & Puzzles Thura Review: The Star-Blessed Hunter Guide

Is Thura still a top-tier hunter or just a trophy on the wall? We analyze her unique +80% Special Skill damage buff, her bypass mechanics, and whether this fast-speed ranger can still compete with the modern power-crept monsters of 2026.
Thura: The Star-Blessed Sharpshooter of the Plains
Thura: The Sharpshooter Who Makes Everyone Else Look Better
Thura is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the

family, originally released in May 2024 during the Covenant Summon. In an era where heroes often try to do everything (heal, taunt, and damage), Thura sticks to what Rangers do best: hunting specific targets and making sure they stay down.

. While she can dish out respectable damage herself, her true value lies in her ability to act as a "force multiplier" for your other heroes. By providing a massive Special Skill damage buff and a crucial Bypass capability, she sets the table for her teammates to feast.

Thura’s design screams "Apex Predator." Clad in tribal hunter gear with a massive, serrated bow, she looks like she spends her weekends tracking gargantuan beasts across the savannah rather than sipping tea in the Stronghold. The artwork captures a sense of focused dynamism—she isn't posing; she's stalking. The vibrant yellow and green palette ties her perfectly to the Plains Hunter theme, standing out against the darker, grittier tones of many recent heroes. It’s refreshing to see a hero who looks like she actually needs to aim.

This is the crown jewel. Most attack buffs add to the "Attack" stat (which suffers from diminishing returns). Thura buffs the

While 435% to three targets is good, modern heroes often hit for 500%+ to one or 400%+ to all. Thura relies on her buffs to compete.
If Thura fires before a sniper who normally deals 600% damage, that sniper effectively hits for a significantly higher threshold, often enough to one-shot even Limit Broken tanks.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Plains Hunter family is all about aggression, and Thura's kit reflects that perfectly.

bonus provides a flat Attack increase (5% to 20%) based on the number of family members. While nice, you likely won't run a full team of them. Even a solo bonus is absent, so she relies on her base stats unless paired.
A 10% chance to drop received special damage to 1. This is pure RNG. When it triggers, it saves the match; when it doesn't, she dies. Do not rely on it for strategy, treat it as a lucky bonus.

Applies a DoT that deals 150 damage over 3 turns (refreshable to 1500). Honestly, 50 damage per turn is negligible in the current meta of 3000+ HP heroes. It’s flavor text more than a killer mechanic.
is the standard offensive Aether power. It’s simple and effective. It helps her keep up with the stat inflation of newer heroes, ensuring her "Guiding Arrow" still stings.

Upon release, Thura was a premier solution to the "Counter-Attack" and "Defense Up" meta. She was a staple in top-tier raid attacks because she allowed you to bypass the annoying defenses of heroes like Hachiko or the Gargoyles.
Her direct damage has fallen off. Hitting three targets for ~400% is no longer "lethal" on its own against modern defensive stats. However, her
Thura has transitioned from a primary carry to a
. You don't bring her to kill the team solo; you bring her to ensure your
killer (like a modern slow hero or a monster sniper) connects without being blocked by defensive buffs. She is essential for Titans and specific Raid combos.
Thura is a Ranger, and her primary role is to deal damage and ensure her teammates kill.
Maximizes her scaling damage (435% of a higher attack stat is significant). Increases the potency of her tile damage for Titans.
Leaves her squishy (low base defense). She might die before firing in high-level wars.
Keeps her alive to cast her crucial buffs.
is the way to go. If she isn't hitting hard, she's just a buffer, and there are sturdier buffers out there. Rely on her speed to fire before she dies.
Ignores Defense Up, Counter-attack, and Elemental Defense buffs. Critical for dealing with Toons and heavily buffed tanks.
The 80% Special Skill damage boost is rare and stacks multiplicatively with standard attack buffs.
High tile damage + Bypass + Buff makes her a staple for Purple Titans.
Fast mana allows her to set the pace of the match.
Her defensive stats make her a glass cannon in 2026.
Her DoT passive is incredibly weak and virtually irrelevant.
Her survival relies on a 10% passive chance, which is unreliable.
The Attack stat, the damage buff, and the ability to bypass defensive buffs on the Titan make her a top pick.
Excellent for cracking "Turtle" defenses that stack buffs.
While she is fast, in Rush, her buff allows your Slow smashers (like Alfrike or Khufu) to wipe the enemy team even harder.
The AI doesn't time her buffs well, and she is too squishy to survive focused sniper fire.
Thura is a "Setup" hero. She must fire
Thura strips the enemy defenses (bypass) and applies the +80% skill damage buff. The subsequent multi-hitters then deal nearly double their usual damage, bypassing any defensive measures.
Thura ensures the Titan's defensive buffs (if any) are ignored, and her special damage buff stacks with Miki's normal attack buff for massive ability damage.
heroes prevent her from hitting the high-health targets she wants.
(like Hel or Proteus) shut her down completely.
Do not pair her with heroes who overwrite her buffs with weaker versions (though +Special Skill Damage is a rare buff slot, so this is uncommon).
A Fast 5-star who hits three targets will carry you through Season 2, 3, and 4 map stages effortlessly. Her ability to bypass counter-attacks will save you many headaches in Raid Tournaments.
You likely have heroes with better stats. However, do not sleep on her for

. The +80% Special Skill damage buff is a key ingredient for high scores. If you have her, she is worth the scopes if you lack a dedicated "Damage Amplifier," but maybe skip the Alpha Aethers unless you use her daily.
When Thura was released, the community was divided. Some called her "Just another hitter," while others recognized the potential of her buff.
"She allows her teammates to gain bypass and special attack up... She is one of the highest special attack buffers in the game by a longshot."
"I love her. Place two fast snipers next to her and they bypass softskin with the extra damage."
The consensus has shifted from seeing her as a damage dealer to valuing her as a
Thura is the arrow that guides the rest of your team to victory. She may not have the hull-busting stats of the latest Construct or Astral heroes, but her kit provides something stats can't buy:
She opens the door, and lets your other heroes walk through it with a bazooka.
A specialized tool that remains sharp despite its age. Keep her for the offense, bench her for the defense.
